name: Bible
description: Stories, figures, and books from the Old and New Testaments

questions.q: In what year was the King James Version of the Bible first published?

source.notes: Commissioned by King James I in 1604. Also called the Authorized Version.

questions.q: How many books are in the Old Testament (Protestant canon)?

source.notes: Catholic OT has 46 books; Protestant 39. New Testament has 27. Total Protestant: 66.

questions.q: What is the name of the first book of the Bible?

source.notes: From Greek 'genesis' meaning 'origin' or 'creation.' Covers creation through Joseph in Egypt.

questions.q: According to the Bible, how many rivers flowed out of the Garden of Eden?

source.notes: Pishon, Gihon, Tigris, and Euphrates (Genesis 2:10-14).

questions.q: Who is the first person killed in the Bible?

source.notes: Killed by his brother Cain out of jealousy (Genesis 4).

questions.q: Who was betrayed by his brothers and sold into slavery, famous for his 'coat of many colors'?

source.notes: Son of Jacob. Later became vizier of Egypt. Subject of the musical 'Joseph and the Amazing Technicolor Dreamcoat.'

questions.q: Who built the Ark to survive the Great Flood?

source.notes: Genesis 6-9. Name means 'rest' in Hebrew. Took animals 'two by two.'

questions.q: Who led the Israelites out of Egypt in the biblical Exodus?

source.notes: Exodus follows Genesis. Moses received the Ten Commandments at Mount Sinai.

questions.q: According to the Gospels, which criminal was released instead of Jesus before the crucifixion?

source.notes: Passover custom allowed Pilate to release one prisoner. The crowd chose Barabbas.

questions.q: Who delivered the first Christian sermon on the Day of Pentecost?

source.notes: Acts 2. About 3,000 people converted. Marks the 'birthday of the church.'

questions.q: Which Psalm begins 'The Lord is my shepherd, I shall not want'?

source.notes: Attributed to King David. Commonly read at funerals.

questions.q: How many disciples did Jesus choose as his closest apostles?

source.notes: Number mirrors the 12 tribes of Israel. Judas replaced by Matthias after the betrayal.

questions.q: What are the first four books of the New Testament collectively called?

source.notes: Matthew, Mark, Luke, John. Tell the life and teachings of Jesus.

questions.q: Which two books of the Bible are named after women?

source.notes: Both are in the Old Testament.

questions.q: In the Old Testament, who killed the Philistine giant Goliath with a slingshot?

source.notes: 1 Samuel 17. David was a young shepherd who later became king of Israel.

questions.q: In the famous biblical story, how many days and nights did Jonah spend in the belly of the great fish?

source.notes: Jonah 1:17. Referred to by Jesus in Matthew 12:40.

questions.q: How many commandments did Moses receive on Mount Sinai?

source.notes: Exodus 20 and Deuteronomy 5. Written on two stone tablets.

questions.q: Who were the parents of Jesus according to the New Testament?

source.notes: Mary was a virgin. Joseph was a carpenter from Nazareth.

questions.q: Which disciple famously denied Jesus three times before the cock crowed?

source.notes: Simon Peter. Later became 'the rock' on which Jesus built his church.

questions.q: The last book of the Christian Bible is called what?

source.notes: Revelation of John. Attributed to John of Patmos. Describes apocalyptic visions.
